concerns.text: FR-001 uses 'predefined complexity limit' instead of the idea's explicit '≤ 13 crossings'. This is a scope drift that reduces verifiability. Replace with '≤ 13 crossings' to match the research question.
FR-003 uses 'high match threshold' without quantification. This makes the requirement untestable. Replace with '≥ 90% match against KnotInfo reference values' per MAINTAINER DECISION.
FR-013 uses 'Reference coverage threshold set to a high level' without quantification. Replace with '≥ 90% of cited references resolve to a verified source' per MAINTAINER DECISION.
SC-005 uses 'A high proportion of knots' without quantification. Replace with '≥ 95% of knots with computable invariants have all invariants populated' per MAINTAINER DECISION.
SC-010 uses 'achieves a high match threshold' without quantification. Replace with '≥ 90% match against reference values' per MAINTAINER DECISION.

SC-011 uses 'families with residuals exceeding a specified threshold' without quantification. Replace with 'families with residuals exceeding ≥ 2 standard deviations from the fitted trend' per MAINTAINER DECISION.

Direct constitutional contradiction: Principle VII requires p-values/confidence intervals for all statistical claims, while FR-006 states 'p-values are NOT reported for census data'. The Constitution itself must be amended to allow this exception, not just the Assumptions section.

FR-003 contains excessive inline caveats ('Phase 1 Implementation Exclusion', 'Phase 2+ Timeline', etc.). Consolidate these into a dedicated Phase 2+ section or Assumptions to reduce maintenance burden.
